units) at resolution","finalYesTotal":"Total YES capital (gwei units) at resolution"}}},"title":"OccultMarket \u2014 Wave 1 Encrypted AMM prediction market on Fhenix CoFHE. Core privacy guarantee:   - yes_pool and no_pool are ciphertexts. No observer can read pool composition.   - placeBet routes to the correct pool via FHE.select \u2014 no branching, no signal.   - Price is only revealed on-chain every PRICE_UPDATE_INTERVAL via threshold decrypt.   - Individual trades are permanently invisible between price updates. Wave 1 limitations (honest):   - Oracle: owner manually resolves with final pool totals (mocked).   - Amounts stored as gwei units in euint32 (max ~4.29 ETH per position).   - Price update and claim are two transactions each (async FHE decrypt).   - Trading pauses briefly during price update window.","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"finalizeClaim(uint256)":{"notice":"Step 3: finalize claim once results are published. Payout = (user_position / winning_pool_total) * total_pool"},"finalizePrice(uint256)":{"notice":"Step 3: finalize price once results are published. params.encryptedDirection: Encrypted bool: true = YES, false = NO

params.finalNoTotal: Total NO capital (gwei units) at resolution
params.finalYesTotal: Total YES capital (gwei units) at resolution

finalizeClaim(uint256).notice: Step 3: finalize claim once results are published. Payout = (user_position / winning_pool_total) * total_pool

finalizePrice(uint256).notice: Step 3: finalize price once results are published. Call after FHE decryption completes (typically a few seconds on testnet).

placeBet(uint256,(uint256,uint8,uint8,by.notice: Place a bet. Direction and amount are encrypted. What leaks on-chain: a bet happened, from this address, at this time. What does NOT leak: direction (YES/NO), amount, or any pool composition. Amount convention: client encrypts (msg.value / 1e9) as gwei units. Max single bet: ~4.29 ETH (uint32 overflow beyond that).

publishClaim(uint256,address,uint32,byte.notice: Step 2: Accept decrypted payout value with Threshold Network proof.

publishPriceUpdate(uint256,uint32,bytes,.notice: Step 2: Accept decrypted pool values with Threshold Network proof.

requestClaim(uint256).notice: Step 1: queue FHE decrypt of your winning position. Call after market is resolved.

requestPriceUpdate(uint256).notice: Step 1: snapshot pool state and queue FHE threshold decryption. Can be called by anyone after PRICE_UPDATE_INTERVAL has elapsed. New bets during the pending window go into live pools and are included in the NEXT price update (not this one).

resolve(uint256,bool,uint256,uint256).notice: Resolve market. Wave 1: owner provides final pool totals manually. This is the mocked oracle — Wave 4 replaces with encrypted multi-voter quorum.
